rcomplex 0.1.0
  • Reference

Identify hub genes that recur across trait-exclusive cliques

Source: R/cliques.R
clique_hubs.Rd

For each gene appearing in the output of find_cliques, counts how many distinct HOGs it participates in and how many of those cliques are trait-exclusive. Genes that appear in conserved cliques across many independent ortholog groups are candidate master determinants of the trait.

clique_hubs(cliques, target_species, species_trait = NULL, min_hogs = 2L)

Arguments

cliques

Output of find_cliques.

target_species

Character vector of species (must match column names in cliques).

species_trait

Optional named character or factor vector mapping species to trait groups (same format as clique_stability). When provided, cliques are annotated by trait exclusivity and per-trait counts are included in the output.

min_hogs

Minimum number of HOG appearances to include a gene in the output (default 2).

Value

A data frame sorted by n_exclusive (or n_hogs if no trait provided), with columns:

gene

Gene identifier

species

Species the gene belongs to

n_hogs

Number of distinct HOGs where this gene appears in a clique

n_exclusive

Number of those cliques that are trait-exclusive (only present when species_trait is provided)

n_

One column per trait level counting exclusive clique appearances (only present when species_trait is provided)

Contents
